name: HRC_EVT_HANDLERS
description:
An Event Handler identifies how a particular event will be managed once it is determined that its criteria has been satisfied.

Detection of an event can result in
a) an event being published to the SOA server (payload is dependent on the type of event)
b) a standard event (i.e. the payload is a fixed shape) is published to the SOA server; this can represent either a data change, in which case the standard event will contain the Source Object Change ID, or a transaction framework based event, in which case the payload will contain the Transaction ID
c) an internal call to a synchronous process, allowing invocation of processing in the same transaction
d) storage of data in the Source Object Change tables without publishing an event to SOA.

columns.Name: EVENT_HANDLER_ID
columns.Comments: Unique Identifier

columns.Name: EVENT_TYPE_ID
columns.Comments: Identifier of Event Type

columns.Name: CONSTRUCTION_METHOD_ID
columns.Comments: Identifier of a Construction Method

columns.Name: HANDLER_TYPE
columns.Comments: Indicator of whether the event handler is "Internal" or "External"

columns.Name: PRODUCT_CODE
columns.Comments: Product Code used for filtering of events

columns.Name: PRODUCT_LABEL
columns.Comments: Free text label to allow multiple event handlers within a product for the same event

columns.Name: CLASS_NAME
columns.Comments: The name of an internal Java class used to invoke synchronous event processing

columns.Name: PROPAGATE_FUSION_CONTEXT
columns.Comments: Propagate Fusion Context to External Service.

columns.Name: SERVICE_URL
columns.Comments: The External Service URL to be called.

columns.Name: CSF_KEY
columns.Comments: Credential Store Factory Key.

columns.Name: MESSAGE_ENCRYPTION_KEY
columns.Comments: Message Encryption Key.

columns.Name: OWSM_POLICY
columns.Comments: Oracle Web Service Client Policy.

columns.Name: NOTIFY_FLAG
columns.Comments: Determines whether an event is published to the SOA server to notify the product that the event has occurred

columns.Name: ENABLED
columns.Comments: Determines whether the event handler is currently enabled

columns.Name: MODULE_ID
columns.Comments: Seed Data Framework: indicates the module that owns the row. A module is an entry in Application Taxonomy such as a Logical Business Area. When the MODULE_ID column exists and the owner of the row is not specified, then the Seed Data Framework will not extract the row as seed data.

columns.Name: ENTERPRISE_ID
columns.Comments: Identifier of Enterprise, used for multi-tenancy partitioning. Foreign key to HR_ORGANIZATION_UNITS.

columns.Name: OBJECT_VERSION_NUMBER
columns.Comments: Used to implement optimistic locking. This number is incremented every time that the row is updated. The number is compared at the start and end of a transaction to detect whether another session has updated the row since it was queried.

columns.Name: CREATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who created the row.

columns.Name: CREATION_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the creation of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the last update of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_LOGIN
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the session login associated to the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: OBJ_CONDITION_ID
columns.Comments: Identifier of Event Object Condition

columns.Name: SGUID
columns.Comments: The seed global unique identifier. Oracle internal use only.

columns.Name: SEED_DATA_SOURCE
columns.Comments: Source of seed data record. A value of 'BULK_SEED_DATA_SCRIPT' indicates that record was bulk loaded. Otherwise, specifies the name of the seed data file.

columns.Name: ORA_SEED_SET1
columns.Comments: Oracle internal use only. Indicates the edition-based redefinition (EBR) context of the row for SET1. Context values are Y or N.

columns.Name: ORA_SEED_SET2
columns.Comments: Oracle internal use only. Indicates the edition-based redefinition (EBR) context of the row for SET2. Context values are Y or N.

indexes.Columns: EVENT_TYPE_ID, CONSTRUCTION_METHOD_ID, PRODUCT_CODE, PRODUCT_LABEL, ENTERPRISE_ID, SERVICE_URL, OBJ_CONDITION_ID, ORA_SEED_SET1
EVENT_TYPE_ID, CONSTRUCTION_METHOD_ID, PRODUCT_CODE, PRODUCT_LABEL, ENTERPRISE_ID, SERVICE_URL, OBJ_CONDITION_ID, ORA_SEED_SET2
